rationale: A better matches SinkConnector’s actual contract: connector-level metadata, task factory/config generation, and sink-runtime neighbors like SinkTask/WorkerSinkTask. B is more URL-grounded, but its test anchor is clearly off-target (ExactlyOnceSourceIntegrationTest) and it leans on a generic Connector framing instead of the sink-specific API surface.

A: Test anchors are plausible but not verified as exact neighboring Kafka 4.2.0 sink tests.
It omits direct versioned doc URLs, so grounding is weaker than B.
B: Cites an unrelated source integration test as the neighboring test anchor.
Overstates the usefulness of generic Connector guidance instead of anchoring SinkConnector-specific responsibilities and tests.
